input.plan:
# ElibertyBookingV2 provider implementation

## Context

The booking team has shipped a new REST API to replace the legacy Eliberty booking backend. We need a new provider `ElibertyBookingV2` that plugs into the existing `ExternalActivityHandlerInterface` so bookable products can be served by the new backend without changing the funnel or other providers.

The new backend is split across **two distinct hosts**:

- **Super-admin backend** (`https://superadmin-backend.skiwise.com`) — hosts ONLY `POST /login`, which returns the JWT used everywhere else. Documented in `booking-admin-api.json`.
- **Booking module** (`https://booking-module.skiwise.com`) — hosts EVERY other endpoint, including the ones prefixed `/admin/*` (e.g. `GET /admin/timeslot/list/{resourceUuid}`) and all `/client/booking/*` calls. Documented in `booking-api-swagger.json`.

Both calls use the same Bearer JWT (per user decision) issued by `/login` on the super-admin host. Every protected call (on the booking-module host) also requires the multi-tenant header `x-tenant-slag`, whose value is read from ConfigManager key `booking.v2.tenant-slag`.

The new provider must satisfy the same `ExternalActivityHandlerInterface` contract as `AxBooking`, `ElibertyBooking`, `Koralp`, `TeamaxessReservation`, so the existing `BookingService` / `BookingHelper` / cart funnel keep working unchanged. Existing providers stay untouched.

PHP 7.4. All code must pass phpcs, phpstan level 1, and ship with PHPUnit unit tests using the `MockHandler`-based pattern already in `tests/Unit/.../ElibertyBookingHandlerTest.php`.

## Architectural decisions (locked)

1. **Auth**: a single JWT obtained from `POST /login` is used as `Authorization: Bearer` on both admin and client endpoints.
2. **synchronizeBooking** = `POST /client/booking/prebook/extend/{bookingUuid}` only. No booker mutation between prebook and confirm.
3. **createBooking** payload uses real Order data with `BaseApiHelper` fallback chain (firstname/lastname/email/phone), defaulting to existing constants when missing.
4. **No refactor** of existing providers / `BaseApiHelper`. All new code lives under `ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2/`.

## Files to create

```
src/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2/
└── Rest/
    ├── ClientFactory.php            # Builds JWT-authenticated Guzzle client
    ├── TokenProvider.php            # Login + Redis-cached JWT, re-login on 401
    ├── ApiHelper.php                # Param builders + response → entity mappers
    └── ElibertyBookingV2Handler.php # Implements ExternalActivityHandlerInterface

tests/Unit/Eliberty/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2/
└── Rest/
    ├── ElibertyBookingV2HandlerTest.php
    ├── TokenProviderTest.php
    └── ApiHelperTest.php

tests/Unit/Eliberty/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2/Fixtures/
├── login_ok.json
├── timeslot_list.json
├── booking_create.json
└── booking_confirm.json
```

## Files to modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/RedpillBundle/Enum/ExternalBookingProvider.php` | Add constant `ELIBERTY_BOOKING_V2 = 'eliberty_booking_v2'` and append it to the `$values` array. |
| `src/RedpillBundle/Resources/config/services.yml` | Add 4 service definitions (Handler, ApiHelper, ClientFactory, TokenProvider) with the appropriate bindings. The handler is auto-tagged because the existing interface autoconfiguration adds `external_activity.handler` to anything implementing `ExternalActivityHandlerInterface`. Verify with phpunit factory test. |

No DB migration: the new provider stores nothing new. It re-uses `SubContingent` and `ExternalBooking` entities, distinguished by `type = 'eliberty_booking_v2'`.

## Configuration keys (ConfigManager)

| Key | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `booking.v2.tenant-slag` | string | sent as `x-tenant-slag` header on every booking-module call (key name dictated by spec) |
| `booking.v2.login_base_url` | string | super-admin host, used **only** for `POST /login` (e.g. `https://superadmin-backend.skiwise.com`) |
| `booking.v2.api_base_url` | string | booking-module host, used for ALL other calls — both `/admin/*` and `/client/*` (e.g. `https://booking-module.skiwise.com`) |
| `booking.v2.username` | string | super-admin login |
| `booking.v2.password` | string | super-admin password |
| `booking.v2.cache_expiration_time` | int | timeslot list TTL (default 300, falls back to existing `booking.cache_expiration_time`) |
| `booking.v2.token_safety_margin` | int | seconds subtracted from JWT TTL before treating it as expired (default 30) |

These are read via the existing `ConfigManager::getStringOption()` / `getIntOption()` API. The product still carries the resource UUID in `Product::getExternalActivityResourceId()` (the existing field — the spec confirms `resource_id → resourceUuid`).

## Component design

### `TokenProvider`
Single responsibility: hand out a valid JWT.

- Dependencies: `GuzzleHttp\Client` (built once with `login_base_url` — the super-admin host), `ConfigManager`, `CacheInterface $redisCache` (`@generic_cache`), `LoggerInterface $loggerBooking`.
- Public API: `getToken(): string`, `invalidate(): void`.
- Cache key: `md5('booking_v2_jwt_' . tenantSlag . '_' . username)`.
- On miss / expired: `POST /login` with `{username, password, tenantSlag}` → cache `access_token` with TTL `expires_in - safety_margin`.
- Logs success/failure on the `booking` channel; throws `ExternalActivityException` on failed login (so calling handler can wrap it consistently).

> Rationale: keeping token logic out of the handler is the only abstraction we add. We do **not** introduce a new shared JWT helper across providers (per refactor scope decision).

### `ClientFactory`
- Exposes a single `getClient(): Client` returning a `GuzzleHttp\Client` configured with `base_uri = booking.v2.api_base_url` (the booking-module host). This single client serves BOTH the `/admin/timeslot/list/...` and `/client/booking/...` calls — they live on the same host.
- Pushes a small middleware stack that:
  1. Adds `Authorization: Bearer <TokenProvider::getToken()>`
  2. Adds `x-tenant-slag: <ConfigManager::getStringOption('booking.v2.tenant-slag')>`
  3. On 401 response → `TokenProvider::invalidate()`, retry **once**.
- The retry-on-401 middleware is the only "magic" — implemented as a simple closure pushed onto the `HandlerStack`, not a new framework.
- `TokenProvider` keeps its own internal Guzzle client pointed at `login_base_url` so the login call never goes through this middleware (avoids infinite retry loops on bad credentials).

### `ApiHelper`
Mirrors the role of existing per-provider `ApiHelper` classes; extends `BaseApiHelper` to inherit booker fallback / logging / cache key helpers.

Responsibilities:

- `buildCreateBookingPayload(Order, SubContingentData): array` — assembles the `RequestCreateBookingDto` body. Uses `BaseApiHelper::getBookerName('firstname'/'lastname', ...)`, `getBookerPhone(...)`, plus the order's email. `bookingDetail.productId = $product->getCode()`, `pricePaid` in cents from the matching order item, `currency = 'EUR'` (constant for now — flag if multi-currency needed), `origin = 'ESHOP'`, `orderDate = today`, `customerUserId = (string) $order->getId()`.
- `buildConfirmBookingPayload(Order, ExternalBooking): array` — same `bookingDetail` shape, re-using stored ExternalBooking data when available.
- `mapTimeslotToSubContingent(array $timeslot, Product, WebInstance): SubContingent` — finds-or-creates by `externalId = $timeslot['uuid']`, sets `quotaAvailable = capacity - occupiedSlots`, `quotaInUse = occupiedSlots`, `date` from `startDate + startTime` (minutes), `dailyBooking = false`, `type = ELIBERTY_BOOKING_V2`.
- `mapBookingResponseToExternalBooking(array $response, ?SubContingent, ?ExternalBooking): ExternalBooking` — sets `externalId = $response['uuid']`, status from `isCanceled` (`true` → CANCELLED, `false` → INIT/CART/CONFIRMED depending on caller), copies booker fields.
- `getDateRangeForAvailability(Product): array{from:string,to:string}` — re-uses the existing `Season::getCurrentSeason()` pattern from `getDaysAvailabilityTrait` (read it: `src/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/Traits/getDaysAvailabilityTrait.php:1-39`).

### `ElibertyBookingV2Handler` — interface mapping

| Interface method | Implementation |
|---|---|
| `getDaysAvailability(Product)` | `GET /admin/timeslot/list/{resourceUuid}?dateFrom=...&dateTo=...` on the booking-module client. Return distinct `startDate` values. **Cached** in Redis by `getRedisKey()`. |
| `getSubContingentsBetweenDates(Product, $start, $end)` | Same endpoint, narrower range. Map every timeslot via `ApiHelper::mapTimeslotToSubContingent()`. **Cached**. |
| `getSubContingent(Product, $date, $id, $withCache)` | Try DB lookup by `(externalId=$id, webinstance, type=v2)`; on miss, call `getSubContingentsBetweenDates` for that single day and re-query DB. |
| `createBooking(Order, SubContingentData)` | `POST /client/booking/{timeSlotUuid}` with `isItPreBooking=true` and the payload from `buildCreateBookingPayload`. Map response → ExternalBooking with status INIT, persist. |
| `synchronizeBooking(Order, ExternalBooking)` | `POST /client/booking/prebook/extend/{bookingUuid}`. Update `updatedAt`. Status stays as is. |
| `confirmBooking(Order, ExternalBooking)` | `POST /client/booking/prebook/confirm/{bookingUuid}` with `buildConfirmBookingPayload`. Set status CONFIRMED. |
| `findBooking($externalBookingId, WebInstance, ?$item)` | DB-only lookup (mirrors v1). The new API has no read-by-id endpoint. |

All public methods wrap external errors (`GuzzleHttp\Exception\RequestException`, `\Throwable`) into `ExternalActivityException` via `BaseApiHelper::parseAndLogException()`.

## Existing utilities to reuse (do NOT reimplement)

- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\Helpers\BaseApiHelper` — booker fallback chain, redis key builder, cache TTL, logger.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\Models\{SubContingent, ExternalBooking, SubContingentData}` — the data carriers used by the funnel.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\ExternalActivityHandlerFactory` — auto-resolves the handler by class name from the provider enum string `eliberty_booking_v2` → `ElibertyBookingV2\Rest\ElibertyBookingV2Handler`. Naming MUST match exactly.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\Entity\Season::getCurrentSeason()` — date range for availability (same usage as `getDaysAvailabilityTrait`).
- `@generic_cache` (`Psr\SimpleCache\CacheInterface`) — bound as `$redisCache`.
- `@monolog.logger.booking` — bound as `$loggerBooking`.

## Build sequence

1. **Enum + service skeleton** — add `ELIBERTY_BOOKING_V2` constant; create empty handler class so the factory naming convention test passes.
2. **TokenProvider** + unit test (mock Guzzle MockHandler, mock cache, assert login on miss, no login on hit, re-login on `invalidate()`).
3. **ClientFactory** + unit test (assert headers added, assert one retry on 401).
4. **ApiHelper** + unit test for each mapping function (use fixtures).
5. **Handler** — implement methods one at a time, each with a focused unit test that mocks `ClientFactory::getClient()` / `getAdminClient()` with `MockHandler`. Mirror the structure of `ElibertyBookingHandlerTest`.
6. **Wire services** in `services.yml`; verify with `ExternalActivityHandlerFactoryTest` (extend it to assert the v2 handler resolves).
7. **Run quality gates**: `composer cs-fix && composer stan && composer ci:tests:unit`.

## Verification

Local / CI:

```bash
docker compose exec redpill php vendor/bin/phpunit --configuration config/phpunit.xml.dist \
  --filter ElibertyBookingV2

docker compose exec redpill php vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer fix --config=.php-cs-fixer.dist.php --dry-run --diff \
  src/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2

docker compose exec redpill php vendor/bin/phpstan analyse -c phpstan.neon.dist -l 1 \
  src/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2
```

2. Mark a test product `externalBookingProvider = 'eliberty_booking_v2'` and set its `externalActivityResourceId` to a real `resourceUuid`.
3. Hit the funnel that lists availabilities (existing controller calling `BookingService`) — confirm timeslots are returned and `SubContingent` rows are persisted with `type = eliberty_booking_v2`.
4. Add the product to a cart → confirm `POST /client/booking/{uuid}` is hit, `ExternalBooking` row created, status `INIT`.
5. Trigger sync (cart refresh) → confirm `/prebook/extend/...` call.
6. Pay the order → confirm `/prebook/confirm/...` call and status flips to `CONFIRMED`.
7. Force a 401 (invalidate the cached token in Redis) → confirm middleware re-logins transparently.

- `currency = 'EUR'` is hardcoded; if any tenant uses another currency, surface a TODO and a config knob.
- The new API has no `findBooking` by ID endpoint; if the funnel ever needs to recover a lost booking from the remote system, this will need a follow-up.

# ApiClient Infrastructure - Complete Analysis for ElibertyBookingV2 Integration

I've completed an exhaustive exploration of the ApiClient infrastructure. Here's everything you need to build the new ElibertyBookingV2 client:

## 1. DIRECTORY TREE

The infrastructure is organized under `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/`:

```
ApiClient/
├── Infra/                          # Core reusable infrastructure
│   ├── Client/
│   │   ├── ApiClientInterface.php
│   │   ├── AuthJwtApiClient.php
│   │   ├── BaseApiClient.php
│   │   ├── BaseHttpClient.php
│   │   └── ParameterizedClientFactory.php
│   ├── Error/Token/
│   │   ├── TokenErrorInterface.php
│   │   ├── StatusCodeTokenError.php
│   │   ├── ApiPlatformTokenError.php
│   │   └── ApiIngenieTokenError.php
│   ├── Param/
│   │   ├── ApiParametersInterface.php
│   │   ├── ApiClientParametersInterface.php
│   │   ├── ApiClientParameters.php
│   │   ├── ApiParameters.php
│   │   └── ApiConfigParameters.php
│   ├── Response/
│   │   ├── ApiResponseInterface.php
│   │   ├── ApiResponseErrorInterface.php
│   │   ├── ApiResponse.php
│   │   └── ApiResponseError.php
│   └── Token/
│       ├── ApiTokenInterface.php
│       ├── TokenInterface.php
│       ├── TokenCacheKey.php
│       ├── JwtToken.php
│       └── RedisApiToken.php
├── DciPump/, Ingenie/, IoMedia/, Trinity/  # Concrete implementations
```

---

## 2. CORE INTERFACES & CLASSES

### ApiClientInterface
**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Infra/Client/ApiClientInterface.php` (Lines 12-20)

Single method contract:
```php
public function call(string $method, string $uri = '', array $options = []): ApiResponseInterface;
```

### ApiResponseInterface
**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Infra/Response/ApiResponseInterface.php` (Lines 10-30)

Methods:
- `getCode(): int` — HTTP status code
- `getData(): array` — Response body parsed as array
- `getValue(string $key)` — Get specific key from response
- `getUrl(): string` — Called URL
- `isValueMatch(string $key, array $match): bool` — Check if value in array

### ApiResponseErrorInterface
**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Infra/Response/ApiResponseErrorInterface.php` (Lines 10-13)

Extends ApiResponseInterface:
- `getErrorMesssage(): string` — Human-readable error (note: typo in original code)

---

## 3. AUTHJWTAPICLIENT - FULL AUTHENTICATION FLOW

**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Infra/Client/AuthJwtApiClient.php` (Lines 1-197)

### Constructor (Lines 61-75)
```php
public function __construct(
    HttpClientInterface $client,
    ApiParametersInterface $apiParameters,
    ApiTokenInterface $apiToken,                    // RedisApiToken injected
    TokenErrorInterface $tokenError,               // Strategy for detecting token errors
    LoggerInterface $logger,
    IdentityService $identityService              // For multi-tenant header
)
```

### Main call() Method (Lines 90-117)
The orchestration method that implements full token lifecycle:

1. **Line 92** — Set hash key from parameters: `$this->apiToken->setHashKey($this->getParameters());`
   - Hash key computed from credentials (except password) + URI
   
2. **Line 93-94** — Check/login: `if (!$this->apiToken->hasValidToken()) { $this->login(); }`
   - Checks if valid token exists in Redis cache
   - If not, calls private `login()` method
   
3. **Line 97** — Make authenticated call: `$apiResponse = $this->authenticatedCall($method, $uri, $options);`
   - Adds auth header with cached token
   - Makes actual HTTP request
   
4. **Line 100-101** — Success path: `if (!$apiResponse instanceof ApiResponseErrorInterface) { return $apiResponse; }`
   - If 2xx/3xx response, return immediately
   
5. **Line 105** — Token error detection: `if (!$this->tokenError->isInvalidToken($apiResponse))`
   - Delegates to TokenErrorInterface implementation
   - If NOT token error → throw exception (4xx or 5xx)
   
6. **Line 113-116** — Token expired path: Re-login and single retry
   - `$this->login();`
   - `return $this->authenticatedCall($method, $uri, $options);`

### login() Method (Lines 130-162)
Handles credential-based authentication:

**Required Parameters (throwError=true):**
- `login_uri: string` — Endpoint to POST credentials
- `credentials: array` — ['username' => '...', 'password' => '...']

**Optional Parameters:**
- `response_token_key: string` (default: `'token'`) — Key to extract JWT from response
- `login_data_key: string` (default: `'json'`) — How to send credentials (`json` or `form_params`)
- `login_multi_tenant_header_key: string` — Header key for multi-tenant (if ends with `-id`, sends webinstance ID, else shortname)

**Flow:**
1. **Lines 132-136** — Gather parameters from ApiParameters
2. **Line 138** — Build options: `$options = [$loginDataKey => $credentials];`
3. **Lines 140-143** — Optional multi-tenant header injection
4. **Line 147** — POST to login endpoint: `$apiResponse = $this->apiClient->call('POST', $loginUri, $options);`
5. **Lines 154-158** — Extract token: `$newToken = $apiResponse->getValue($responseTokenKey);`
6. **Line 161** — Store in Redis: `$this->apiToken->setToken($newToken);`

### authenticatedCall() Method (Lines 175-196)
Injects token into request:

1. **Lines 177-178** — Build URL: `$callUri = $this->getStringParameter('main_uri') ?? ''; $callUri .= $uri;`
2. **Lines 180-182** — Merge default options
3. **Line 184-185** — Add auth header: `$options[$clientTokenOptionKey] = $this->apiToken->getToken();`
   - Key default: `'auth_bearer'` (becomes `auth_bearer` in Symfony HttpClient)
4. **Line 188** — Make HTTP call: `$this->apiClient->call($method, $callUri, $options);`

---

## 4. TOKEN MANAGEMENT

### ApiTokenInterface
**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Infra/Token/ApiTokenInterface.php` (Lines 1-43)

```php
public function setToken(string $token): self;
public function getToken(): ?string;
public function hasValidToken(): bool;
public function setHashKey(array $parameters): void;
public function getHashKey(): ?string;
```

### RedisApiToken
**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Infra/Token/RedisApiToken.php` (Lines 1-90)

**Constructor (Lines 23-31):**
```php
public function __construct(
    CacheInterface $redisCache,        // PSR-16 cache adapter (Redis)
    TokenInterface $token,             // JwtToken instance
    LoggerInterface $logger
)
```

**setToken() (Lines 38-55):**
```php
public function setToken(string $token): self
{
    // Verify hash key exists (line 40-41)
    if (null === $this->getHashKey()) {
        throw new ApiTokenException('Token hash key need to be set...');
    }

    // Compute TTL (remaining seconds) from JWT
    $ttl = $this->token->set($token)->getTtl();  // Line 44

    // Store in Redis with auto-expiration
    $this->cache->set($this->getHashKey(), $token, $ttl);  // Line 47
    
    $this->logger->debug('Token store into redis cache', ['token' => $token]);
    return $this;
}
```

```php
public function getToken(): ?string
{
    if (null === $this->getHashKey()) {
        throw new ApiTokenException('Token hash key need to be set...');
    }

    $cachedToken = $this->cache->get($this->getHashKey());  // Line 69

    // Verify token still valid (not expired)
    if (\is_string($cachedToken) && !$this->token->set($cachedToken)->isExpired()) {  // Line 74
        $this->logger->debug('Valid token found in cache', ['token' => $cachedToken]);
        return $cachedToken;
    }

    return null;
}
```

**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Infra/Token/TokenCacheKey.php` (Lines 1-78)

```php
public function setHashKey(array $parameters): void
{
    $apiDiscriminantValues = $this->getDiscriminant($parameters);
    if ($this->discriminantValues !== $apiDiscriminantValues) {
        $this->discriminantValues = $apiDiscriminantValues;
        
        // MD5 hash of discriminant
        $this->hashKey = CacheUtils::computeKey($this->discriminantValues);  // Line 35
    }
}
```

**getDiscriminant() (Lines 56-77):**
Extracts:
1. Credentials (except keys in `cache_keys_excluded`, default: `['password']`)
2. Discriminant URI: tries `discriminant_uri` → `base_uri` → `main_uri`

Example result:
```php

    'username' => 'user@example.com',
    'email' => 'user@example.com',
    'uri' => 'https://api.example.com/v1'
```

```php
public function set(string $token): self
{
    $this->token = $token;

    // Decode JWT header (line 30)
    /** @var array{exp: int} $decodedToken */
    $decodedToken = JWTHelper::decodeToken($this->token);

    // Extract expiration timestamp (Unix time)
    $this->expirationTime = $decodedToken['exp'] ?? 0;  // Line 35

    return $this;
}
```

**getTtl() (Lines 65-72):**

```php
public function getTtl(): ?int
{
    if ($this->expirationTime) {
        // Line 68: Remaining seconds = exp claim - current time
        return $this->expirationTime - time();
    }

    return null;
}
```

```php
public function isExpired(int $ttlSafetyMargin = 10): bool
{
    if ($this->expirationTime) {
        // Line 56: Expired if TTL <= safety margin (10s default to avoid race conditions)
        return $ttlSafetyMargin >= $this->getTtl();
    }

    return false;
}
```

```php
class BaseTrinityApiClient
{
    protected static array $requestOptions = [];
    private ApiClientInterface $trinityApiClient;

    public function __construct(ApiClientInterface $trinityApiClient)
    {
        $this->trinityApiClient = $trinityApiClient;
    }

    protected function trinityApiCall(string $method, string $url, array $options = []): array
    {
        return $this->trinityApiClient->call(
            $method,
            $url,
            array_merge(static::$requestOptions, $options)
        )->getData();
    }
}
```

### ContactTrinityApiClient

```php
class ContactTrinityApiClient extends BaseTrinityApiClient
{
    protected static array $requestOptions = [
        'timeout' => 10, // seconds max
    ];

    public function getContactByExternalId(string $externalId, int $saleschannelId, bool $force = false): array
    {
        $url = sprintf('contacts/getByExternalId/%s/%s', $saleschannelId, $externalId);
        if (true === $force) {
            $url .= '/force';
        }

        return $this->trinityApiCall('GET', $url);
    }
}
```

**Pattern:**
1. Inject `ApiClientInterface` in constructor
2. Call `$this->client->call(METHOD, URI, OPTIONS)->getData()`
3. Handle errors by checking response code or letting exceptions propagate

## 10. COMPLETE AUTH FLOW LIFECYCLE

### First Call → No Token → Login → Cache → Success

```
1. User calls: $authJwtApiClient->call('GET', '/contacts', [])

2. AuthJwtApiClient::call() - Line 90
   ├─ Set hash key: $apiToken->setHashKey(params)
   │  └─ Compute MD5 from credentials + URI
   │
   ├─ Check valid token: if (!$apiToken->hasValidToken())
   │  ├─ RedisApiToken::hasValidToken() → RedisApiToken::getToken()
   │  ├─ cache.get(hashKey) → NULL (first time)
   │  └─ hasValidToken = FALSE
   │
   ├─ LOGIN REQUIRED: $this->login()
   │  ├─ Get params: login_uri, credentials, response_token_key, etc.
   │  ├─ Build: options = ['json' => credentials]
   │  ├─ Call: POST login_uri with credentials
   │  │  └─ BaseApiClient::call() → HTTP request
   │  │     └─ Returns ApiResponse(200, {'token': 'eyJ...'}, url)
   │  ├─ Extract: newToken = response.getValue('token')
   │  └─ Store: $apiToken->setToken(newToken)
   │     ├─ JwtToken::set() → Decode JWT → extract exp claim
   │     ├─ getTtl() = exp - time() = 3600 seconds (example)
   │     └─ cache.set(hashKey, token, 3600)
   │        └─ Redis auto-expires after 3600 seconds
   │
   ├─ Make auth call: $authenticatedCall('GET', '/contacts', [])
   │  ├─ Get token: apiToken->getToken()
   │  │  └─ cache.get(hashKey) → 'eyJ...'
   │  ├─ Add header: options['auth_bearer'] = token
   │  └─ HTTP call: client->request('GET', url, options)
   │     └─ Returns ApiResponse(200, data, url)
   │
   └─ Return response ✓

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

3. Token Cached - Next Call Uses Cache

   AuthJwtApiClient::call('GET', '/other-endpoint', [])
   ├─ Set hash key: same as before
   ├─ Check token: $apiToken->hasValidToken()
   │  ├─ cache.get(hashKey) → 'eyJ...' (from Redis)
   │  ├─ JwtToken::isExpired() → FALSE (TTL > 10 seconds)
   │  └─ return TRUE
   │
   ├─ Token valid → Skip login
   ├─ Make auth call with cached token
   └─ Return response ✓

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

4. Server Returns 401 - Token Expired

   AuthJwtApiClient::call('GET', '/endpoint', [])
   ├─ Token exists in cache (not time-expired locally)
   ├─ Make call with token
   │  └─ Server returns: ApiResponseError(401, error_data, url)
   │
   ├─ Check if error response: YES
   ├─ Check if token error: $tokenError->isInvalidToken(response)
   │  └─ StatusCodeTokenError: 401 === code → TRUE
   │
   ├─ IS token error → RE-LOGIN
   │  ├─ login() again
   │  ├─ Get new token
   │  └─ Store in Redis (overwrite old)
   │
   ├─ RETRY once: authenticatedCall() with fresh token
   │  └─ Should succeed now
   │
   └─ Return response ✓

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

5. Server Returns 404 - Non-Auth Error

   AuthJwtApiClient::call('GET', '/nonexistent', [])
   ├─ Token valid
   ├─ Make call
   │  └─ Response: ApiResponseError(404, error, url)
   │
   ├─ Check error: YES
   ├─ Check token error: isInvalidToken() → FALSE (404 ≠ 401)
   │
   ├─ NOT token error → THROW EXCEPTION
   │  ├─ if (500 > 404): throw ApiClientException('...', 404)
   │  └─ else: throw ApiRetryableException('...', xxx)
   │
   └─ Exception propagates to caller
```

---

## 11. CACHE KEY COMPUTATION

**File:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/Util/CacheUtils.php` (Lines 14-28)

```php
public static function computeKey($input)
{
    // Example:
    // Input: ['email' => 'user@example.com', 'uri' => 'https://api.example.com']
    
    // Step 1: Convert to 'key_value' strings
    $datas = array_map(fn($v, $k) => $k.'_'.$v, $input, array_keys($input));
    // Result: ['email_user@example.com', 'uri_https://api.example.com']

    // Step 2: Join with underscore
    $joined = implode('_', $datas);
    // Result: 'email_user@example.com_uri_https://api.example.com'

    // Step 3: MD5 hash
    return md5($joined);
    // Result: '7f8d9c2a1b4e6f3a9c8d7b6e5f4a3c2d'
}
```

## 12. PATTERN FOR NEW ELIBERTYBOOKINGV2 CLIENT

To build the new client, follow this exact pattern:

### Step 1: Create TokenError Strategy

```php
final class ElibertyBookingV2TokenError implements TokenErrorInterface
{
    public function isInvalidToken(ApiResponseErrorInterface $apiResponseError): bool
    {
        // Match your API's auth error response format
        return 401 === $apiResponseError->getCode()
            && $apiResponseError->isValueMatch('status', ['UNAUTHORIZED', 'TOKEN_EXPIRED']);
    }
}
```

### Step 2: Wire in services.yml

```php
class BookingApiRepository
{
    public function __construct(
        ApiClientInterface $bookingApiClient,
    ) {
        $this->client = $bookingApiClient;
    }

    public function getBookings(array $filters = []): array
    {
        return $this->client->call(
            'GET',
            '/bookings',
            ['query' => $filters]
        )->getData();
    }

    public function createBooking(array $data): array
    {
        return $this->client->call(
            'POST',
            '/bookings',
            ['json' => $data]
        )->getData();
    }
}

```

This infrastructure handles all JWT authentication, token caching with Redis, automatic re-login on token expiration, multi-tenant support, and parameter injection from ConfigManager. Everything is production-tested across Ingenie, Trinity, Pricehub, DciPump, and other APIs.

## 3. AUTHJWTAPICLIENT - FULL AUTHENTICATION FLOW

**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Infra/Client/AuthJwtApiClient.php` (Lines 1-197)

### Constructor (Lines 61-75)
```php
public function __construct(
    HttpClientInterface $client,
    ApiParametersInterface $apiParameters,
    ApiTokenInterface $apiToken,                    // RedisApiToken injected
    TokenErrorInterface $tokenError,               // Strategy for detecting token errors
    LoggerInterface $logger,
    IdentityService $identityService              // For multi-tenant header
)
```

### Main call() Method (Lines 90-117)
The orchestration method that implements full token lifecycle:

1. **Line 92** — Set hash key from parameters: `$this->apiToken->setHashKey($this->getParameters());`
   - Hash key computed from credentials (except password) + URI
   
2. **Line 93-94** — Check/login: `if (!$this->apiToken->hasValidToken()) { $this->login(); }`
   - Checks if valid token exists in Redis cache
   - If not, calls private `login()` method
   
3. **Line 97** — Make authenticated call: `$apiResponse = $this->authenticatedCall($method, $uri, $options);`
   - Adds auth header with cached token
   - Makes actual HTTP request
   
4. **Line 100-101** — Success path: `if (!$apiResponse instanceof ApiResponseErrorInterface) { return $apiResponse; }`
   - If 2xx/3xx response, return immediately
   
5. **Line 105** — Token error detection: `if (!$this->tokenError->isInvalidToken($apiResponse))`
   - Delegates to TokenErrorInterface implementation
   - If NOT token error → throw exception (4xx or 5xx)
   
6. **Line 113-116** — Token expired path: Re-login and single retry
   - `$this->login();`
   - `return $this->authenticatedCall($method, $uri, $options);`

### login() Method (Lines 130-162)
Handles credential-based authentication:

**Required Parameters (throwError=true):**
- `login_uri: string` — Endpoint to POST credentials
- `credentials: array` — ['username' => '...', 'password' => '...']

**Optional Parameters:**
- `response_token_key: string` (default: `'token'`) — Key to extract JWT from response
- `login_data_key: string` (default: `'json'`) — How to send credentials (`json` or `form_params`)
- `login_multi_tenant_header_key: string` — Header key for multi-tenant (if ends with `-id`, sends webinstance ID, else shortname)

**Flow:**
1. **Lines 132-136** — Gather parameters from ApiParameters
2. **Line 138** — Build options: `$options = [$loginDataKey => $credentials];`
3. **Lines 140-143** — Optional multi-tenant header injection
4. **Line 147** — POST to login endpoint: `$apiResponse = $this->apiClient->call('POST', $loginUri, $options);`
5. **Lines 154-158** — Extract token: `$newToken = $apiResponse->getValue($responseTokenKey);`
6. **Line 161** — Store in Redis: `$this->apiToken->setToken($newToken);`

### authenticatedCall() Method (Lines 175-196)
Injects token into request:

1. **Lines 177-178** — Build URL: `$callUri = $this->getStringParameter('main_uri') ?? ''; $callUri .= $uri;`
2. **Lines 180-182** — Merge default options
3. **Line 184-185** — Add auth header: `$options[$clientTokenOptionKey] = $this->apiToken->getToken();`
   - Key default: `'auth_bearer'` (becomes `auth_bearer` in Symfony HttpClient)
4. **Line 188** — Make HTTP call: `$this->apiClient->call($method, $callUri, $options);`

### RedisApiToken
**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Infra/Token/RedisApiToken.php` (Lines 1-90)

**Constructor (Lines 23-31):**


**setToken() (Lines 38-55):**
```php
public function setToken(string $token): self
{
    // Verify hash key exists (line 40-41)
    if (null === $this->getHashKey()) {
        throw new ApiTokenException('Token hash key need to be set...');
    }

    // Compute TTL (remaining seconds) from JWT
    $ttl = $this->token->set($token)->getTtl();  // Line 44

    // Store in Redis with auto-expiration
    $this->cache->set($this->getHashKey(), $token, $ttl);  // Line 47
    
    $this->logger->debug('Token store into redis cache', ['token' => $token]);
    return $this;
}
```

**getToken() (Lines 62-81):**
```php
public function getToken(): ?string
{
    if (null === $this->getHashKey()) {
        throw new ApiTokenException('Token hash key need to be set...');
    }

    $cachedToken = $this->cache->get($this->getHashKey());  // Line 69

    // Verify token still valid (not expired)
    if (\is_string($cachedToken) && !$this->token->set($cachedToken)->isExpired()) {  // Line 74
        $this->logger->debug('Valid token found in cache', ['token' => $cachedToken]);
        return $cachedToken;
    }

    return null;
}
```

**hasValidToken() (Lines 86-89):**
```php
public function hasValidToken(): bool
{
    return (bool) $this->getToken();
}
```

### TokenCacheKey
**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Infra/Token/TokenCacheKey.php` (Lines 1-78)

**setHashKey() (Lines 29-37):**
```php
public function setHashKey(array $parameters): void
{
    $apiDiscriminantValues = $this->getDiscriminant($parameters);
    if ($this->discriminantValues !== $apiDiscriminantValues) {
        $this->discriminantValues = $apiDiscriminantValues;
        
        // MD5 hash of discriminant
        $this->hashKey = CacheUtils::computeKey($this->discriminantValues);  // Line 35
    }
}
```

**getDiscriminant() (Lines 56-77):**
Extracts:
1. Credentials (except keys in `cache_keys_excluded`, default: `['password']`)
2. Discriminant URI: tries `discriminant_uri` → `base_uri` → `main_uri`

Example result:
```php
[
    'username' => 'user@example.com',
    'email' => 'user@example.com',
    'uri' => 'https://api.example.com/v1'
]
```

### JwtToken
**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Infra/Token/JwtToken.php` (Lines 1-73)

**set() (Lines 24-38):**
```php
public function set(string $token): self
{
    $this->token = $token;

    // Decode JWT header (line 30)
    /** @var array{exp: int} $decodedToken */
    $decodedToken = JWTHelper::decodeToken($this->token);

    // Extract expiration timestamp (Unix time)
    $this->expirationTime = $decodedToken['exp'] ?? 0;  // Line 35

    return $this;
}
```

**getTtl() (Lines 65-72):**
```php
public function getTtl(): ?int
{
    if ($this->expirationTime) {
        // Line 68: Remaining seconds = exp claim - current time
        return $this->expirationTime - time();
    }

    return null;
}
```

**isExpired() (Lines 53-60):**
```php
public function isExpired(int $ttlSafetyMargin = 10): bool
{
    if ($this->expirationTime) {
        // Line 56: Expired if TTL <= safety margin (10s default to avoid race conditions)
        return $ttlSafetyMargin >= $this->getTtl();
    }

    return false;
}
```

## 6. CONCRETE CLIENT EXAMPLE: TRINITY API

### BaseTrinityApiClient
**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Trinity/Resources/BaseTrinityApiClient.php` (Lines 10-29)

```php
class BaseTrinityApiClient
{
    protected static array $requestOptions = [];
    private ApiClientInterface $trinityApiClient;

    public function __construct(ApiClientInterface $trinityApiClient)
    {
        $this->trinityApiClient = $trinityApiClient;
    }

    protected function trinityApiCall(string $method, string $url, array $options = []): array
    {
        return $this->trinityApiClient->call(
            $method,
            $url,
            array_merge(static::$requestOptions, $options)
        )->getData();
    }
}
```

### ContactTrinityApiClient
**Path:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Trinity/Resources/ContactTrinityApiClient.php` (Lines 8-27)

```php
class ContactTrinityApiClient extends BaseTrinityApiClient
{
    protected static array $requestOptions = [
        'timeout' => 10, // seconds max
    ];

    public function getContactByExternalId(string $externalId, int $saleschannelId, bool $force = false): array
    {
        $url = sprintf('contacts/getByExternalId/%s/%s', $saleschannelId, $externalId);
        if (true === $force) {
            $url .= '/force';
        }

        return $this->trinityApiCall('GET', $url);
    }
}
```

**Pattern:**
1. Inject `ApiClientInterface` in constructor
2. Call `$this->client->call(METHOD, URI, OPTIONS)->getData()`
3. Handle errors by checking response code or letting exceptions propagate

## 10. COMPLETE AUTH FLOW LIFECYCLE

### First Call → No Token → Login → Cache → Success

```
1. User calls: $authJwtApiClient->call('GET', '/contacts', [])

2. AuthJwtApiClient::call() - Line 90
   ├─ Set hash key: $apiToken->setHashKey(params)
   │  └─ Compute MD5 from credentials + URI
   │
   ├─ Check valid token: if (!$apiToken->hasValidToken())
   │  ├─ RedisApiToken::hasValidToken() → RedisApiToken::getToken()
   │  ├─ cache.get(hashKey) → NULL (first time)
   │  └─ hasValidToken = FALSE
   │
   ├─ LOGIN REQUIRED: $this->login()
   │  ├─ Get params: login_uri, credentials, response_token_key, etc.
   │  ├─ Build: options = ['json' => credentials]
   │  ├─ Call: POST login_uri with credentials
   │  │  └─ BaseApiClient::call() → HTTP request
   │  │     └─ Returns ApiResponse(200, {'token': 'eyJ...'}, url)
   │  ├─ Extract: newToken = response.getValue('token')
   │  └─ Store: $apiToken->setToken(newToken)
   │     ├─ JwtToken::set() → Decode JWT → extract exp claim
   │     ├─ getTtl() = exp - time() = 3600 seconds (example)
   │     └─ cache.set(hashKey, token, 3600)
   │        └─ Redis auto-expires after 3600 seconds
   │
   ├─ Make auth call: $authenticatedCall('GET', '/contacts', [])
   │  ├─ Get token: apiToken->getToken()
   │  │  └─ cache.get(hashKey) → 'eyJ...'
   │  ├─ Add header: options['auth_bearer'] = token
   │  └─ HTTP call: client->request('GET', url, options)
   │     └─ Returns ApiResponse(200, data, url)
   │
   └─ Return response ✓

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

3. Token Cached - Next Call Uses Cache

   AuthJwtApiClient::call('GET', '/other-endpoint', [])
   ├─ Set hash key: same as before
   ├─ Check token: $apiToken->hasValidToken()
   │  ├─ cache.get(hashKey) → 'eyJ...' (from Redis)
   │  ├─ JwtToken::isExpired() → FALSE (TTL > 10 seconds)
   │  └─ return TRUE
   │
   ├─ Token valid → Skip login
   ├─ Make auth call with cached token
   └─ Return response ✓

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

4. Server Returns 401 - Token Expired

   AuthJwtApiClient::call('GET', '/endpoint', [])
   ├─ Token exists in cache (not time-expired locally)
   ├─ Make call with token
   │  └─ Server returns: ApiResponseError(401, error_data, url)
   │
   ├─ Check if error response: YES
   ├─ Check if token error: $tokenError->isInvalidToken(response)
   │  └─ StatusCodeTokenError: 401 === code → TRUE
   │
   ├─ IS token error → RE-LOGIN
   │  ├─ login() again
   │  ├─ Get new token
   │  └─ Store in Redis (overwrite old)
   │
   ├─ RETRY once: authenticatedCall() with fresh token
   │  └─ Should succeed now
   │
   └─ Return response ✓

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

5. Server Returns 404 - Non-Auth Error

   AuthJwtApiClient::call('GET', '/nonexistent', [])
   ├─ Token valid
   ├─ Make call
   │  └─ Response: ApiResponseError(404, error, url)
   │
   ├─ Check error: YES
   ├─ Check token error: isInvalidToken() → FALSE (404 ≠ 401)
   │
   ├─ NOT token error → THROW EXCEPTION
   │  ├─ if (500 > 404): throw ApiClientException('...', 404)
   │  └─ else: throw ApiRetryableException('...', xxx)
   │
   └─ Exception propagates to caller
```

## 11. CACHE KEY COMPUTATION

**File:** `/home/xav/Workspace/Rpstack/redpill/src/RedpillBundle/Util/CacheUtils.php` (Lines 14-28)

```php
public static function computeKey($input)
{
    // Example:
    // Input: ['email' => 'user@example.com', 'uri' => 'https://api.example.com']
    
    // Step 1: Convert to 'key_value' strings
    $datas = array_map(fn($v, $k) => $k.'_'.$v, $input, array_keys($input));
    // Result: ['email_user@example.com', 'uri_https://api.example.com']

    // Step 2: Join with underscore
    $joined = implode('_', $datas);
    // Result: 'email_user@example.com_uri_https://api.example.com'

    // Step 3: MD5 hash
    return md5($joined);
    // Result: '7f8d9c2a1b4e6f3a9c8d7b6e5f4a3c2d'
}
```

## 12. PATTERN FOR NEW ELIBERTYBOOKINGV2 CLIENT

To build the new client, follow this exact pattern:

### Step 1: Create TokenError Strategy
**File:** `src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/Infra/Error/Token/ElibertyBookingV2TokenError.php`

```php
final class ElibertyBookingV2TokenError implements TokenErrorInterface
{
    public function isInvalidToken(ApiResponseErrorInterface $apiResponseError): bool
    {
        // Match your API's auth error response format
        return 401 === $apiResponseError->getCode()
            && $apiResponseError->isValueMatch('status', ['UNAUTHORIZED', 'TOKEN_EXPIRED']);
    }
}
```

```yaml
eliberty_booking_v2.api.client.jwt.authenticated:
    class: Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Client\AuthJwtApiClient
    factory: ['@Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Client\ParameterizedClientFactory', 'createAuthJwtApiClient']
    arguments:
        $tokenError: '@Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Error\Token\ElibertyBookingV2TokenError'
        $apiParams:
            base_uri: '%env(ELIBERTY_BOOKING_V2_API_BASE_URL)%'
            login_uri: /api/auth/token
            credentials:
                api_key: '%env(ELIBERTY_BOOKING_V2_API_KEY)%'
                api_secret: '%env(ELIBERTY_BOOKING_V2_API_SECRET)%'
```

### Step 3: Create Repository
**File:** `src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/ElibertyBookingV2/Repository/BookingApiRepository.php`

```php
class BookingApiRepository
{
    public function __construct(
        ApiClientInterface $bookingApiClient,
    ) {
        $this->client = $bookingApiClient;
    }

    public function getBookings(array $filters = []): array
    {
        return $this->client->call(
            'GET',
            '/bookings',
            ['query' => $filters]
        )->getData();
    }

    public function createBooking(array $data): array
    {
        return $this->client->call(
            'POST',
            '/bookings',
            ['json' => $data]
        )->getData();
    }
}
```

### Step 4: Wire Repository
```yaml
Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\ElibertyBookingV2\Repository\BookingApiRepository:
    arguments:
        $bookingApiClient: '@eliberty_booking_v2.api.client.jwt.authenticated'
```

---

This infrastructure handles all JWT authentication, token caching with Redis, automatic re-login on token expiration, multi-tenant support, and parameter injection from ConfigManager. Everything is production-tested across Ingenie, Trinity, Pricehub, DciPump, and other APIs.
Got it. Rewriting the plan to use the `ApiClient` infrastructure (AuthJwtApiClient + ParameterizedClientFactory) instead of raw Guzzle.

# ElibertyBookingV2 provider implementation

## Context

The booking team has shipped a new REST API to replace the legacy Eliberty booking backend. We need a new provider `ElibertyBookingV2` that plugs into the existing `ExternalActivityHandlerInterface` so bookable products can be served by the new backend without changing the funnel or other providers.

The new backend is split across **two distinct hosts**:
- **Super-admin backend** (`https://superadmin-backend.skiwise.com`) — hosts ONLY `POST /login`, which returns the JWT used everywhere else. Documented in `booking-admin-api.json`.
- **Booking module** (`https://booking-module.skiwise.com`) — hosts EVERY other endpoint, including the ones prefixed `/admin/*` (e.g. `GET /admin/timeslot/list/{resourceUuid}`) and all `/client/booking/*` calls. Documented in `booking-api-swagger.json`.

Both hosts accept the same Bearer JWT issued by `/login`. Every call to the booking-module host must also carry the multi-tenant header `x-tenant-slag`, whose value is read from ConfigManager key `booking.v2.tenant-slag`.

The new provider must satisfy the same `ExternalActivityHandlerInterface` contract as `AxBooking`, `ElibertyBooking`, `Koralp`, `TeamaxessReservation`, so the existing `BookingService` / `BookingHelper` / cart funnel keep working unchanged. Existing providers stay untouched.

PHP 7.4. All code must pass phpcs, phpstan level 1, and ship with PHPUnit unit tests.

## Architectural decisions (locked)

1. **Auth**: a single JWT obtained from `POST /login` (super-admin host) is sent as `Authorization: Bearer` on all booking-module calls.
2. **HTTP layer**: MUST use the existing `src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient` infrastructure (`AuthJwtApiClient` + `ParameterizedClientFactory` + `RedisApiToken` + `JwtToken`). No raw Guzzle. No new token cache. No new client factory.
3. **synchronizeBooking** = `POST /client/booking/prebook/extend/{bookingUuid}` only. No booker mutation between prebook and confirm.
4. **createBooking** payload uses real Order data via `BaseApiHelper` booker fallback chain, defaulting to existing constants when missing.
5. **No refactor** of existing providers / `BaseApiHelper`. All new code lives under `ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2/` and `ApiClient/ElibertyBookingV2/`.

## Files to create

```
src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/ElibertyBookingV2/
├── Client/
│   └── ElibertyBookingV2ApiClient.php   # Decorator: adds x-tenant-slag header on every call
└── Repository/
    └── BookingV2Repository.php          # Thin repo: one method per remote endpoint, returns arrays

src/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2/
└── Rest/
    ├── ApiHelper.php                    # Param builders + response→entity mappers
    └── ElibertyBookingV2Handler.php     # Implements ExternalActivityHandlerInterface

tests/Unit/Eliberty/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/ElibertyBookingV2/
├── Client/ElibertyBookingV2ApiClientTest.php
└── Repository/BookingV2RepositoryTest.php

tests/Unit/Eliberty/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2/
└── Rest/
    ├── ElibertyBookingV2HandlerTest.php
    └── ApiHelperTest.php
```

## Files to modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/RedpillBundle/Enum/ExternalBookingProvider.php` | Add `ELIBERTY_BOOKING_V2 = 'eliberty_booking_v2'` and append to `$values` array. |
| `src/RedpillBundle/Resources/config/services.yml` | Add 4 service definitions: the underlying `AuthJwtApiClient` (built via `ParameterizedClientFactory::createAuthJwtApiClient`), the `ElibertyBookingV2ApiClient` decorator, the `BookingV2Repository`, and the `ElibertyBookingV2Handler`. The handler is auto-tagged via the existing autoconfiguration of `ExternalActivityHandlerInterface`. |

No DB migration: re-uses `SubContingent` and `ExternalBooking` entities, distinguished by `type = 'eliberty_booking_v2'`.

## Configuration keys (ConfigManager)

| Key | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `booking.v2.tenant-slag` | string | sent as `x-tenant-slag` header on every booking-module call |
| `booking.v2.api_base_url` | string | booking-module host, e.g. `https://booking-module.skiwise.com` |
| `booking.v2.login_url` | string | absolute URL to `POST /login`, e.g. `https://superadmin-backend.skiwise.com/login` |
| `booking.v2.credentials` | json | `{"username": "...", "password": "..."}` (consumed by `ApiConfigParameters` as a single `credentials` value) |
| `booking.v2.cache_expiration_time` | int | timeslot list TTL (default falls back to existing `booking.cache_expiration_time`) |

## Component design

### `ApiClient/ElibertyBookingV2/Client/ElibertyBookingV2ApiClient`

Lightweight decorator implementing `ApiClientInterface`. Its only job is to inject the `x-tenant-slag` header into every call before delegating to the underlying `AuthJwtApiClient` (which already handles JWT login, Redis token caching, TTL from JWT exp claim, and 401-retry).

```php
final class ElibertyBookingV2ApiClient implements ApiClientInterface
{
    public function __construct(
        private ApiClientInterface $authClient,   // wired to the AuthJwtApiClient service
        private ConfigManager $configManager
    ) {}

    public function call(string $method, string $uri = '', array $options = []): ApiResponseInterface
    {
        $tenantSlag = $this->configManager->getStringOption('booking.v2.tenant-slag');
        $options['headers']['x-tenant-slag'] = $tenantSlag;
        return $this->authClient->call($method, $uri, $options);
    }
}
```

> Why a decorator and not `$apiParams['options']['headers']`: the tenant-slag must be read from ConfigManager (per-tenant), not baked into a static service-container parameter. The decorator is the smallest correct seam.

### `ApiClient/ElibertyBookingV2/Repository/BookingV2Repository`

Mirrors the existing `Trinity\Resources\BaseTrinityApiClient` style: a thin wrapper around `ApiClientInterface` that exposes one PHP method per remote endpoint and returns associative arrays. No business logic, no entity mapping.

```php
class BookingV2Repository
{
    public function __construct(private ApiClientInterface $bookingV2ApiClient) {}

    public function listTimeslots(string $resourceUuid, string $dateFrom, string $dateTo): array
    {
        return $this->bookingV2ApiClient->call(
            'GET',
            sprintf('/admin/timeslot/list/%s', $resourceUuid),
            ['query' => ['dateFrom' => $dateFrom, 'dateTo' => $dateTo]]
        )->getData();
    }

    public function createBooking(string $timeslotUuid, array $payload): array
    {
        return $this->bookingV2ApiClient->call(
            'POST',
            sprintf('/client/booking/%s', $timeslotUuid),
            ['json' => $payload]
        )->getData();
    }

    public function extendPrebook(string $bookingUuid): array
    {
        return $this->bookingV2ApiClient->call(
            'POST',
            sprintf('/client/booking/prebook/extend/%s', $bookingUuid)
        )->getData();
    }

    public function confirmPrebook(string $bookingUuid, array $payload): array
    {
        return $this->bookingV2ApiClient->call(
            'POST',
            sprintf('/client/booking/prebook/confirm/%s', $bookingUuid),
            ['json' => $payload]
        )->getData();
    }
}
```

Throws naturally: `AuthJwtApiClient` raises `ApiClientException` / `ApiRetryableException` on non-2xx that isn't a token error. The handler catches and rewraps as `ExternalActivityException`.

### `ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2/Rest/ApiHelper`

Extends the existing `BaseApiHelper` to inherit the booker fallback chain, redis cache key helper, cache TTL accessor, logger. Pure mapping/serialization, no HTTP.

Methods:

- `buildCreateBookingPayload(Order, SubContingentData): array` — assembles the `RequestCreateBookingDto` body.
  - `isItPreBooking = true`
  - `customerUserId = (string) $order->getId()`
  - `customerName = trim(getBookerName('firstname', ...) . ' ' . getBookerName('lastname', ...))` (uses base helper fallback chain)
  - `customerEmail = $order->getEmail() ?? ExternalBooking::DEFAULT_NAME . '@local'`
  - `customerPhoneNumber = getBookerPhone(...)`
  - `bookingDetail = { productName, productId: $product->getCode(), pricePaid (cents), currency: 'EUR', orderDate: today, origin: 'ESHOP' }`
- `buildConfirmBookingPayload(Order, ExternalBooking): array` — re-emits the same `bookingDetail` shape.
- `mapTimeslotToSubContingent(array $timeslot, Product, WebInstance): SubContingent` — find-or-create by `externalId = $timeslot['uuid']`, sets `quotaAvailable = capacity - occupiedSlots`, `quotaInUse = occupiedSlots`, `date = startDate + startTime minutes`, `dailyBooking = false`, `type = ELIBERTY_BOOKING_V2`. Persists via injected `EntityManagerInterface`.
- `mapBookingResponseToExternalBooking(array $response, ?SubContingent, ?ExternalBooking): ExternalBooking` — sets `externalId = $response['uuid']`, status from `$response['isCanceled']`.
- `getDateRangeForAvailability(Product): array{from:string, to:string}` — re-uses the season pattern from `src/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/Traits/getDaysAvailabilityTrait.php`.

### `ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2/Rest/ElibertyBookingV2Handler`

Implements `ExternalActivityHandlerInterface`. Constructor injects `BookingV2Repository`, `ApiHelper` (the new one), `CacheInterface $redisCache`, `EntityManagerInterface`, `LoggerInterface $loggerBooking`.

Interface method mapping:

| Interface method | Implementation |
|---|---|
| `getDaysAvailability(Product)` | Compute `[dateFrom, dateTo]` via `ApiHelper::getDateRangeForAvailability`. Cache by `getRedisKey('booking_v2_days', $resourceUuid, $params)`. On miss, `$repository->listTimeslots(...)`, return distinct `startDate` values. |
| `getSubContingentsBetweenDates(Product, $start, $end)` | Same `listTimeslots` call with the requested range. Map every timeslot via `ApiHelper::mapTimeslotToSubContingent()`. **Cached**. |
| `getSubContingent(Product, $date, $id, $withCache)` | Try DB lookup `(externalId=$id, webinstance, type=v2)`; on miss, call `getSubContingentsBetweenDates` for that single day and re-query DB. |
| `createBooking(Order, SubContingentData)` | `$repository->createBooking($subContingent->getExternalId(), $apiHelper->buildCreateBookingPayload(...))`. Map response → ExternalBooking with status INIT. Persist. |
| `synchronizeBooking(Order, ExternalBooking)` | `$repository->extendPrebook($externalBooking->getExternalId())`. Status untouched. |
| `confirmBooking(Order, ExternalBooking)` | `$repository->confirmPrebook($externalBooking->getExternalId(), $apiHelper->buildConfirmBookingPayload(...))`. Status → CONFIRMED. |
| `findBooking($externalBookingId, WebInstance, ?$item)` | DB-only lookup (mirrors v1; the new API has no read-by-id endpoint). |

All public methods wrap external errors (`ApiClientException`, `ApiRetryableException`, `\Throwable`) into `ExternalActivityException` via `BaseApiHelper::parseAndLogException()`.

## Service wiring (services.yml)

```yaml
# Underlying authenticated JWT client built by the existing factory.
# login_url is absolute and points to the super-admin host;
# main_uri/base_uri points to the booking-module host.
eliberty_booking_v2.api.client.jwt.authenticated:
    class: Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Client\AuthJwtApiClient
    factory: ['@Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Client\ParameterizedClientFactory', 'createAuthJwtApiClient']
    arguments:
        $tokenError: '@Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Error\Token\StatusCodeTokenError'
        $apiParams:
            login_data_key: json
            response_token_key: access_token
            cache_keys_excluded: ['password']
        $apiConfigParams:
            base_uri:    [booking.v2.api_base_url]
            main_uri:    [booking.v2.api_base_url]
            login_uri:   [booking.v2.login_url]
            credentials: [booking.v2.credentials]

# Decorator that injects x-tenant-slag from ConfigManager.
Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\ElibertyBookingV2\Client\ElibertyBookingV2ApiClient:
    arguments:
        $authClient: '@eliberty_booking_v2.api.client.jwt.authenticated'
        $configManager: '@Eliberty\ConfigManagerBundle\Model\ConfigManager'

# Repository — exposed to the handler.
Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\ElibertyBookingV2\Repository\BookingV2Repository:
    arguments:
        $bookingV2ApiClient: '@Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\ElibertyBookingV2\Client\ElibertyBookingV2ApiClient'

# ApiHelper (extends existing BaseApiHelper, picks up its bindings).
Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\ElibertyBookingV2\Rest\ApiHelper: ~

# Handler — auto-tagged because of the existing
# `ExternalActivityHandlerInterface: tags: [external_activity.handler]`
# entry; the GenericTagCompilerPass adds it to the factory.
Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\ElibertyBookingV2\Rest\ElibertyBookingV2Handler: ~
```

The handler service ID MUST be the FQCN above so the existing `ExternalActivityHandlerFactory::getServiceId('eliberty_booking_v2')` resolution works. Name conversion: `'eliberty_booking_v2'` → `'ElibertyBookingV2'` → `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\ElibertyBookingV2\Rest\ElibertyBookingV2Handler`.

## Existing utilities to reuse (do NOT reimplement)

- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Client\AuthJwtApiClient` — JWT login + Redis token cache + 401 retry. Configured via `ParameterizedClientFactory::createAuthJwtApiClient`.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Client\ParameterizedClientFactory` — service factory.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Token\{RedisApiToken, JwtToken}` — token storage with TTL parsed from the JWT `exp` claim.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Error\Token\StatusCodeTokenError` — `401 → re-login` strategy. The new API does not document a richer error envelope, so plain status-code matching is sufficient. If smoke tests show false positives we can add a dedicated `ElibertyBookingV2TokenError` later.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\Helpers\BaseApiHelper` — booker fallback chain, redis key builder, cache TTL, logger.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\Models\{SubContingent, ExternalBooking, SubContingentData}` — funnel data carriers.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\ExternalActivityHandlerFactory` — auto-resolves the handler by class name.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\Entity\Season::getCurrentSeason()` — date range for availability.
- `@generic_cache` (`Psr\SimpleCache\CacheInterface`) — bound as `$redisCache`.
- `@monolog.logger.booking` — bound as `$loggerBooking`.

## Build sequence

1. **Enum entry** — add `ELIBERTY_BOOKING_V2` to `ExternalBookingProvider`.
2. **Decorator client** `ElibertyBookingV2ApiClient` + unit test (assert header injected, assert delegation, assert decorator preserves caller-supplied options).
3. **Service wiring** for the JWT client + decorator (via `ParameterizedClientFactory`). Verify with a small functional bootstrap test that the container compiles.
4. **`BookingV2Repository`** + unit test (mock `ApiClientInterface`, assert correct method/uri/options on each call, assert response data is forwarded).
5. **`ApiHelper`** + unit test for each mapping function with fixture arrays.
6. **`ElibertyBookingV2Handler`** — implement methods one at a time, each with a focused unit test that mocks `BookingV2Repository`. Mirror the structure of `tests/Unit/Eliberty/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/IoMedia/BenefitApiClientTest.php` for the mocking style.
7. **Extend `ExternalActivityHandlerFactoryTest`** to assert `getHandler('eliberty_booking_v2')` resolves the new class.
8. **Run quality gates**: `composer cs-fix && composer stan && composer ci:tests:unit`.

## Verification

```bash
docker compose exec redpill php vendor/bin/phpunit --configuration config/phpunit.xml.dist \
  --filter ElibertyBookingV2

docker compose exec redpill php vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer fix --config=.php-cs-fixer.dist.php --dry-run --diff \
  src/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2 \
  src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/ElibertyBookingV2

docker compose exec redpill php vendor/bin/phpstan analyse -c phpstan.neon.dist -l 1 \
  src/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2 \
  src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/ElibertyBookingV2
```

End-to-end smoke (manual, on a dev tenant configured with the v2 keys):

1. Set `booking.v2.*` keys in ConfigManager for a dev webinstance (`tenant-slag`, `api_base_url`, `login_url`, `credentials`).
2. Mark a test product `externalBookingProvider = 'eliberty_booking_v2'` and set its `externalActivityResourceId` to a real `resourceUuid`.
3. Call the funnel that lists availabilities (existing controller calling `BookingService`) — confirm timeslots come back and `SubContingent` rows are persisted with `type = eliberty_booking_v2`. Inspect Redis for the cached JWT token under the AuthJwtApiClient hash key.
4. Add the product to a cart → confirm `POST /client/booking/{uuid}` is hit, `ExternalBooking` row created with status `INIT`.
5. Trigger sync (cart refresh) → confirm `/prebook/extend/...` call.
6. Pay the order → confirm `/prebook/confirm/...` call and status flips to `CONFIRMED`.
7. Force a 401 (delete the cached token in Redis) → confirm `AuthJwtApiClient` re-logins transparently and the next call succeeds.

## Open items to flag during implementation

- `currency = 'EUR'` is hardcoded; surface a TODO if any tenant uses another currency.
- The new API has no `findBooking` by ID endpoint; if the funnel ever needs to recover a lost booking remotely this becomes a follow-up.
- `customerUserId = (string) $order->getId()`. If the v2 backend expects a stable cross-system identifier, revisit during smoke test.
- If the booking module returns 401 with a richer error body, swap `StatusCodeTokenError` for a dedicated `ElibertyBookingV2TokenError` strategy (one new class, one services.yml line).

```php
class BookingV2Repository
{
    public function __construct(private ApiClientInterface $bookingV2ApiClient) {}

    public function listTimeslots(string $resourceUuid, string $dateFrom, string $dateTo): array
    {
        return $this->bookingV2ApiClient->call(
            'GET',
            sprintf('/admin/timeslot/list/%s', $resourceUuid),
            ['query' => ['dateFrom' => $dateFrom, 'dateTo' => $dateTo]]
        )->getData();
    }

    public function createBooking(string $timeslotUuid, array $payload): array
    {
        return $this->bookingV2ApiClient->call(
            'POST',
            sprintf('/client/booking/%s', $timeslotUuid),
            ['json' => $payload]
        )->getData();
    }

    public function extendPrebook(string $bookingUuid): array
    {
        return $this->bookingV2ApiClient->call(
            'POST',
            sprintf('/client/booking/prebook/extend/%s', $bookingUuid)
        )->getData();
    }

    public function confirmPrebook(string $bookingUuid, array $payload): array
    {
        return $this->bookingV2ApiClient->call(
            'POST',
            sprintf('/client/booking/prebook/confirm/%s', $bookingUuid),
            ['json' => $payload]
        )->getData();
    }
}
```

Throws naturally: `AuthJwtApiClient` raises `ApiClientException` / `ApiRetryableException` on non-2xx that isn't a token error. The handler catches and rewraps as `ExternalActivityException`.

### `ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2/Rest/ApiHelper`

Extends the existing `BaseApiHelper` to inherit the booker fallback chain, redis cache key helper, cache TTL accessor, logger. Pure mapping/serialization, no HTTP.

Methods:

- `buildCreateBookingPayload(Order, SubContingentData): array` — assembles the `RequestCreateBookingDto` body.
  - `isItPreBooking = true`
  - `customerUserId = (string) $order->getId()`
  - `customerName = trim(getBookerName('firstname', ...) . ' ' . getBookerName('lastname', ...))` (uses base helper fallback chain)
  - `customerEmail = $order->getEmail() ?? ExternalBooking::DEFAULT_NAME . '@local'`
  - `customerPhoneNumber = getBookerPhone(...)`
  - `bookingDetail = { productName, productId: $product->getCode(), pricePaid (cents), currency: 'EUR', orderDate: today, origin: 'ESHOP' }`
- `buildConfirmBookingPayload(Order, ExternalBooking): array` — re-emits the same `bookingDetail` shape.
- `mapTimeslotToSubContingent(array $timeslot, Product, WebInstance): SubContingent` — find-or-create by `externalId = $timeslot['uuid']`, sets `quotaAvailable = capacity - occupiedSlots`, `quotaInUse = occupiedSlots`, `date = startDate + startTime minutes`, `dailyBooking = false`, `type = ELIBERTY_BOOKING_V2`. Persists via injected `EntityManagerInterface`.
- `mapBookingResponseToExternalBooking(array $response, ?SubContingent, ?ExternalBooking): ExternalBooking` — sets `externalId = $response['uuid']`, status from `$response['isCanceled']`.
- `getDateRangeForAvailability(Product): array{from:string, to:string}` — re-uses the season pattern from `src/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/Traits/getDaysAvailabilityTrait.php`.

### `ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2/Rest/ElibertyBookingV2Handler`

Implements `ExternalActivityHandlerInterface`. Constructor injects `BookingV2Repository`, `ApiHelper` (the new one), `CacheInterface $redisCache`, `EntityManagerInterface`, `LoggerInterface $loggerBooking`.

Interface method mapping:

| Interface method | Implementation |
|---|---|
| `getDaysAvailability(Product)` | Compute `[dateFrom, dateTo]` via `ApiHelper::getDateRangeForAvailability`. Cache by `getRedisKey('booking_v2_days', $resourceUuid, $params)`. On miss, `$repository->listTimeslots(...)`, return distinct `startDate` values. |
| `getSubContingentsBetweenDates(Product, $start, $end)` | Same `listTimeslots` call with the requested range. Map every timeslot via `ApiHelper::mapTimeslotToSubContingent()`. **Cached**. |
| `getSubContingent(Product, $date, $id, $withCache)` | Try DB lookup `(externalId=$id, webinstance, type=v2)`; on miss, call `getSubContingentsBetweenDates` for that single day and re-query DB. |
| `createBooking(Order, SubContingentData)` | `$repository->createBooking($subContingent->getExternalId(), $apiHelper->buildCreateBookingPayload(...))`. Map response → ExternalBooking with status INIT. Persist. |
| `synchronizeBooking(Order, ExternalBooking)` | `$repository->extendPrebook($externalBooking->getExternalId())`. Status untouched. |
| `confirmBooking(Order, ExternalBooking)` | `$repository->confirmPrebook($externalBooking->getExternalId(), $apiHelper->buildConfirmBookingPayload(...))`. Status → CONFIRMED. |
| `findBooking($externalBookingId, WebInstance, ?$item)` | DB-only lookup (mirrors v1; the new API has no read-by-id endpoint). |

All public methods wrap external errors (`ApiClientException`, `ApiRetryableException`, `\Throwable`) into `ExternalActivityException` via `BaseApiHelper::parseAndLogException()`.

## Service wiring (services.yml)

```yaml
# Underlying authenticated JWT client built by the existing factory.
# login_url is absolute and points to the super-admin host;
# main_uri/base_uri points to the booking-module host.
eliberty_booking_v2.api.client.jwt.authenticated:
    class: Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Client\AuthJwtApiClient
    factory: ['@Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Client\ParameterizedClientFactory', 'createAuthJwtApiClient']
    arguments:
        $tokenError: '@Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Error\Token\StatusCodeTokenError'
        $apiParams:
            login_data_key: json
            response_token_key: access_token
            cache_keys_excluded: ['password']
        $apiConfigParams:
            base_uri:    [booking.v2.api_base_url]
            main_uri:    [booking.v2.api_base_url]
            login_uri:   [booking.v2.login_url]
            credentials: [booking.v2.credentials]

# Decorator that injects x-tenant-slag from ConfigManager.
Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\ElibertyBookingV2\Client\ElibertyBookingV2ApiClient:
    arguments:
        $authClient: '@eliberty_booking_v2.api.client.jwt.authenticated'
        $configManager: '@Eliberty\ConfigManagerBundle\Model\ConfigManager'

# Repository — exposed to the handler.
Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\ElibertyBookingV2\Repository\BookingV2Repository:
    arguments:
        $bookingV2ApiClient: '@Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\ElibertyBookingV2\Client\ElibertyBookingV2ApiClient'

# ApiHelper (extends existing BaseApiHelper, picks up its bindings).
Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\ElibertyBookingV2\Rest\ApiHelper: ~

# Handler — auto-tagged because of the existing
# `ExternalActivityHandlerInterface: tags: [external_activity.handler]`
# entry; the GenericTagCompilerPass adds it to the factory.
Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\ElibertyBookingV2\Rest\ElibertyBookingV2Handler: ~
```

The handler service ID MUST be the FQCN above so the existing `ExternalActivityHandlerFactory::getServiceId('eliberty_booking_v2')` resolution works. Name conversion: `'eliberty_booking_v2'` → `'ElibertyBookingV2'` → `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\ElibertyBookingV2\Rest\ElibertyBookingV2Handler`.

## Existing utilities to reuse (do NOT reimplement)

- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Client\AuthJwtApiClient` — JWT login + Redis token cache + 401 retry. Configured via `ParameterizedClientFactory::createAuthJwtApiClient`.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Client\ParameterizedClientFactory` — service factory.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Token\{RedisApiToken, JwtToken}` — token storage with TTL parsed from the JWT `exp` claim.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ApiClient\Infra\Error\Token\StatusCodeTokenError` — `401 → re-login` strategy. The new API does not document a richer error envelope, so plain status-code matching is sufficient. If smoke tests show false positives we can add a dedicated `ElibertyBookingV2TokenError` later.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\Helpers\BaseApiHelper` — booker fallback chain, redis key builder, cache TTL, logger.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\Models\{SubContingent, ExternalBooking, SubContingentData}` — funnel data carriers.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\ExternalActivity\ExternalActivityHandlerFactory` — auto-resolves the handler by class name.
- `Eliberty\RedpillBundle\Entity\Season::getCurrentSeason()` — date range for availability.
- `@generic_cache` (`Psr\SimpleCache\CacheInterface`) — bound as `$redisCache`.
- `@monolog.logger.booking` — bound as `$loggerBooking`.

## Build sequence

1. **Enum entry** — add `ELIBERTY_BOOKING_V2` to `ExternalBookingProvider`.
2. **Decorator client** `ElibertyBookingV2ApiClient` + unit test (assert header injected, assert delegation, assert decorator preserves caller-supplied options).
3. **Service wiring** for the JWT client + decorator (via `ParameterizedClientFactory`). Verify with a small functional bootstrap test that the container compiles.
4. **`BookingV2Repository`** + unit test (mock `ApiClientInterface`, assert correct method/uri/options on each call, assert response data is forwarded).
5. **`ApiHelper`** + unit test for each mapping function with fixture arrays.
6. **`ElibertyBookingV2Handler`** — implement methods one at a time, each with a focused unit test that mocks `BookingV2Repository`. Mirror the structure of `tests/Unit/Eliberty/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/IoMedia/BenefitApiClientTest.php` for the mocking style.
7. **Extend `ExternalActivityHandlerFactoryTest`** to assert `getHandler('eliberty_booking_v2')` resolves the new class.
8. **Run quality gates**: `composer cs-fix && composer stan && composer ci:tests:unit`.

## Verification

```bash
docker compose exec redpill php vendor/bin/phpunit --configuration config/phpunit.xml.dist \
  --filter ElibertyBookingV2

docker compose exec redpill php vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer fix --config=.php-cs-fixer.dist.php --dry-run --diff \
  src/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2 \
  src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/ElibertyBookingV2

docker compose exec redpill php vendor/bin/phpstan analyse -c phpstan.neon.dist -l 1 \
  src/RedpillBundle/ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2 \
  src/RedpillBundle/ApiClient/ElibertyBookingV2
```

End-to-end smoke (manual, on a dev tenant configured with the v2 keys):

1. Set `booking.v2.*` keys in ConfigManager for a dev webinstance (`tenant-slag`, `api_base_url`, `login_url`, `credentials`).
2. Mark a test product `externalBookingProvider = 'eliberty_booking_v2'` and set its `externalActivityResourceId` to a real `resourceUuid`.
3. Call the funnel that lists availabilities (existing controller calling `BookingService`) — confirm timeslots come back and `SubContingent` rows are persisted with `type = eliberty_booking_v2`. Inspect Redis for the cached JWT token under the AuthJwtApiClient hash key.
4. Add the product to a cart → confirm `POST /client/booking/{uuid}` is hit, `ExternalBooking` row created with status `INIT`.
5. Trigger sync (cart refresh) → confirm `/prebook/extend/...` call.
6. Pay the order → confirm `/prebook/confirm/...` call and status flips to `CONFIRMED`.
7. Force a 401 (delete the cached token in Redis) → confirm `AuthJwtApiClient` re-logins transparently and the next call succeeds.

## Open items to flag during implementation

- `currency = 'EUR'` is hardcoded; surface a TODO if any tenant uses another currency.
- The new API has no `findBooking` by ID endpoint; if the funnel ever needs to recover a lost booking remotely this becomes a follow-up.
- `customerUserId = (string) $order->getId()`. If the v2 backend expects a stable cross-system identifier, revisit during smoke test.
- If the booking module returns 401 with a richer error body, swap `StatusCodeTokenError` for a dedicated `ElibertyBookingV2TokenError` strategy (one new class, one services.yml line).

lines: The booking team has shipped a new REST API to replace the legacy Eliberty booking backend. We need a new provider `ElibertyBookingV2` that plugs into the existing `ExternalActivityHandlerInterface` so bookable products can be served by the new backend without changing the funnel or other providers.
The new backend is split across **two distinct hosts**:
- **Super-admin backend** (`https://superadmin-backend.skiwise.com`) — hosts ONLY `POST /login`, which returns the JWT used everywhere else. Documented in `booking-admin-api.json`.

- **Booking module** (`https://booking-module.skiwise.com`) — hosts EVERY other endpoint, including the ones prefixed `/admin/*` (e.g. `GET /admin/timeslot/list/{resourceUuid}`) and all `/client/booking/*` calls. Documented in `booking-api-swagger.json`.

-Both calls use the same Bearer JWT (per user decision) issued by `/login` on the super-admin host. Every protected call (on the booking-module host) also requires the multi-tenant header `x-tenant-slag`, whose value is read from ConfigManager key `booking.v2.tenant-slag`.

+Both hosts accept the same Bearer JWT issued by `/login`. Every call to the booking-module host must also carry the multi-tenant header `x-tenant-slag`, whose value is read from ConfigManager key `booking.v2.tenant-slag`.

The new provider must satisfy the same `ExternalActivityHandlerInterface` contract as `AxBooking`, `ElibertyBooking`, `Koralp`, `TeamaxessReservation`, so the existing `BookingService` / `BookingHelper` / cart funnel keep working unchanged. Existing providers stay untouched.
-PHP 7.4. All code must pass phpcs, phpstan level 1, and ship with PHPUnit unit tests using the `MockHandler`-based pattern already in `tests/Unit/.../ElibertyBookingHandlerTest.php`.
+PHP 7.4. All code must pass phpcs, phpstan level 1, and ship with PHPUnit unit tests.
-1. **Auth**: a single JWT obtained from `POST /login` is used as `Authorization: Bearer` on both admin and client endpoints.
-2. **synchronizeBooking** = `POST /client/booking/prebook/extend/{bookingUuid}` only. No booker mutation between prebook and confirm.
-3. **createBooking** payload uses real Order data with `BaseApiHelper` fallback chain (firstname/lastname/email/phone), defaulting to existing constants when missing.
-4. **No refactor** of existing providers / `BaseApiHelper`. All new code lives under `ExternalActivity/ElibertyBookingV2/`.
